task_completion.description: Construct at least four partition walls and define/name at least four distinct rooms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the SweetHome3D 2D floor plan view, look for at least four newly drawn partition walls and at least four room labels (e.g., Reception, Dining, Business, Lounge).

task_completion.description: Furnish the Reception area with at least 2 desks/counters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least 2 desks or counters placed within the designated Reception area in the floor plan or 3D view.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Dining & Buffet area with at least 4 tables, 8 chairs, and 2 kitchen appliances.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least 4 tables, 8 chairs, and 2 kitchen appliances (e.g., fridge, microwave, oven) placed within the designated Dining area.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Business Center with at least 4 desks and 3 bookcases.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least 4 desks and 3 bookcases placed within the designated Business Center area.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Relaxation Lounge with at least 6 seats, 2 TVs, and 4 decorative plants.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least 6 seats (sofas, armchairs), 2 TVs, and 4 decorative plants placed within the designated Relaxation Lounge area.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Washrooms with at least 3 toilets and 3 sinks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least 3 toilets and 3 sinks placed within the designated Washrooms area.

task_completion.description: Save the updated floor plan over the starter file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the SweetHome3D window title showing 'airport_lounge_starter.sh3d' without an unsaved indicator (like an asterisk), or a save dialog confirming the file is being overwritten.

task_completion.description: Export a 3D photo rendering of the lounge interior to ~/Desktop/lounge_render.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Create photo' or export dialog saving to '~/Desktop/lounge_render.png', or the presence of the 'lounge_render.png' file on the Desktop.

integrity.description: The agent used the SweetHome3D GUI to design the lounge.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the SweetHome3D application open with the agent actively dragging/dropping furniture, drawing walls, or using the application's menus.

integrity.description: The exported render is a genuine 3D photo from the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The render should match the 3D view of the designed lounge in SweetHome3D, rather than being a downloaded image from the internet or a simple screenshot of the 2D plan.

integrity.description: The agent used the provided starter file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The floor plan should show the original structure of the 'airport_lounge_starter.sh3d' file (which may resemble a residential house with a pool) being modified, rather than a completely blank new project.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The starter file is 'airport_lounge_starter.sh3d', which is dynamically generated by stripping furniture from a residential house sample (SweetHome3DExample7.sh3d). It may contain a swimming pool and multiple levels, repurposed as a commercial terminal shell. The agent must use this file.
